cooperates.cooperate_desc: He provides massive crowd control by grouping enemies together, setting up the perfect opportunity for your area-of-effect ultimate to hit multiple targets.
His ability to pull the entire enemy team into one spot allows you to maximize your burst damage and wipe out opponents in seconds.

counters.counter_desc: He offers unparalleled healing and sustain, keeping you alive during intense team fights so you can continue dealing damage without backing away.
She provides global healing and useful buffs that allow you to stay aggressive in your lane and survive unexpected enemy ambushes.

dominants.dominant_desc: Utilize the Bennett (grenade launcher) in the early game to clear minion waves quickly and pressure your lane opponent. Avoid checking bushes alone as Beatrix is easily ambushed, and prioritize engaging in major teamfights only when your Flicker is available for a safe exit. Practice patience by waiting for the right opening rather than rushing in. When using the grenade launcher, always land a basic attack first to trigger the slow effect, making it significantly easier to aim and land your ultimate. Additionally, always keep an eye on your weapon rotation so you aren't caught with the wrong gun during a surprise gank, and try to keep your Renner (sniper) or Wesker (shotgun) ready as a secondary option for quick defensive bursts. Finally, focus on positioning behind your frontline to maximize your damage output while staying out of reach of enemy divers.

emblems.emblem_desc: The Assassin Emblem provides the core penetration needed to melt tanky heroes. Rupture adds extra penetration to pierce heavy defense, while Weapon Master scales your physical attack for massive late-game damage. Weakness Finder completes the set by allowing your basic attacks to slow enemies and reduce their attack speed by 30%, making it easier to kite and finish off targets.

equips.equip_desc: Swift Boots provide early attack speed for faster reloading and fluid attacks, while Corrosion Scythe slows enemies to keep them in range. Golden Staff rapidly triggers on-hit effects, and Malefic Gun extends your reach to poke safely from a distance. Haas Claw adds essential lifesteal for combat sustain, finishing with Blade of Despair for the massive physical damage needed to execute targets.

Swift Boots provide the early attack speed necessary for faster reloading and more fluid basic attacks. Corrosion Scythe is essential for sticking to targets by slowing them down, while Hunter Strike grants physical penetration and a movement speed boost to help you chase or reposition. To handle durable opponents, Sea Halberd increases your damage against high-HP targets and reduces their healing, complemented by Malefic Gun which extends your attack range. Finally, Blade of Despair provides the massive physical damage boost required to execute enemies and complete your lethal build.

hero.hero_overview: A versatile Marksman who uses four unique weapons to adapt to any range or combat situation.
hero.hero_strength: Each of her weapons deals unmatched, massive damage, which becomes even more lethal once she completes her full item build.
hero.hero_weakness: Highly vulnerable to long-range heroes who out-reach her and high-mobility heroes who can easily close the gap and burst her down.

data.recommend: Select this setup to maximize your armor penetration and use your range to melt targets while staying safely behind your team.

spell.spell_desc: Since Beatrix is a glass cannon with only one short dash, Flicker is essential for escaping ambushes or repositioning during a chaotic team fight.

dominants.dominant_desc: In the early game, farm carefully, trying to avoid dying as much as possible. If you're being throttled, ask for active help or try to lane with exp. If you see that your enemy is more passive than you, you can dominate the lane. In the mid-game, you'll gain damage and can actively engage other lanes while still maintaining farm. Also, don't forget about pushing, as Beatrix has fairly good tower damage. You can win the game with just two weapons: a cannon and *******; the sniper rifle and shotgun require some practice. Also, the shotgun is only used after purchasing the green upgrade.

data.title: Adaptive damage

cooperates.cooperate_desc:
· Atlas sets up perfect crowd control with his ultimate.
· Beatrix follows up with Bennett ultimate for massive AoE damage.

· Estes provides constant healing, keeping Beatrix healthy during extended pushes.
· Bennett allows Beatrix to poke safely while Estes sustains her.

counters.counter_desc:
· Chou's knock-up and kick disable Beatrix completely.
· He can kick her into enemy team for instant death.

· Eudora's instant stun combo kills Beatrix before she can move.
· Her burst magic damage ignores physical defense items.
· Beatrix cannot use Flicker while stunned.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Early Game (Levels 1-4)

· Use Bennett to clear waves faster than enemy laners.
· Focus on last hitting minions for gold advantage.
· Bennett outranges turrets - poke enemies safely when they defend.
· Avoid overextending without vision; no escape skill yet.
· Save ultimate for First Turtle contest or securing kills.

Mid Game (Levels 4-8 / First Core Items)

· Push lane aggressively using Bennett's superior wave clear.
· Bennett outranges turrets - damage structures safely.
· After pushing, rotate to help teammates or secure Turtle.
· Use ultimate to zone enemies away from objectives.
· Always place vision in jungle before pushing deep alone.

Late Game (Levels 12-15 / Full Build)

· Split push when safe - Bennett melts turrets from outside range.
· In team fights, use ultimate for massive AoE damage and slow.
· Stick with team during Lord dances, apply side lane pressure.
· Focus on ending game - Bennett destroys structures quickly.
· Save Flicker for escaping assassins during pushes.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Beatrix benefits greatly from the Assassin Emblem with Quantum Charge, which serves as her most balanced and versatile setup. This combination enhances her natural kiting playstyle by granting a 30% movement speed boost whenever she deals damage with basic attacks, allowing her to seamlessly chase down fleeing enemies or create distance from dangerous threats. The additional HP regeneration, scaling from 75 to 180 based on level, provides valuable sustain in the Gold Lane, enabling her to win extended trades and remain on the map longer without being forced to recall. This setup is particularly effective when facing poke-heavy opponents, as the constant healing from basic attacks helps mitigate their harassment while the movement speed keeps her unpredictable.

For players who prefer to dominate the laning phase immediately, the Assassin Emblem with Thrill delivers raw power right from the start. The flat 16 Adaptive Attack significantly boosts her damage output at level one, making her basic attacks and abilities hit considerably harder than the enemy laner expects. This advantage translates into easier last hitting, faster wave clearing, and genuine kill pressure that can secure first blood or force opponents out of lane. The snowball potential from this early strength cannot be overstated, as a Beatrix who gains an early lead becomes nearly unstoppable when she transitions into her core damage items.

Finally, the Marksman Emblem with Weapons Master caters to players who think long-term and want to maximize Beatrix's devastating late-game potential. This talent increases all Physical Attack gained from equipment, emblems, and skills by an additional 5%, which might seem modest early on but becomes terrifying once she completes her full build. When combined with items like Blade of Despair and Malefic Roar, this percentage boost ensures that every Renner sniper shot and Wesker shotgun blast delivers maximum devastation against enemy squishies. This setup is ideal

equips.equip_desc: Buy boots in early sell boots when late game, sky piercer is optional

hero.hero_overview: Beatrix is a versatile Marksman known for mastering multiple weapons. Unlike other heroes, she switches between four unique guns (Renner, Bennett, Wesker, Nibiru), allowing her to adapt to any situation. She is primarily assigned to the Gold Lane to farm efficiently and scale into a powerful late-game damage dealer. Her ability to deal burst damage from long range (Sniper mode) or up close (Shotgun mode) makes her a unpredictable Finisher.
hero.hero_strength:
Beatrix's greatest strength lies in her unparalleled versatility and high burst damage, allowing her to adapt to any combat situation .

· Laning Advantages:
  · Strong Early Game: Beatrix has a powerful early-game presence. She can use Wesker (Shotgun) to clear waves instantly or Nibiru (SMG) to apply constant pressure on the enemy laner .
  · Safe Poking: With Renner (Sniper) , she can poke enemies from a very long range, safely reducing their HP without risking a trade . Both Renner and Bennett (Bazooka) outrange turrets, allowing her to damage structures safely .
  · Flexible Playstyle: Depending on the matchup, she can choose to play aggressively with Wesker or defensively with Renner, making her difficult to counter in the draft phase .
· Team Fight Advantages:
  · High Burst Damage: She excels at eliminating squishy targets (Mages/Marksmen) instantly using Renner's ultimate for long-range picks or Wesker's ultimate for close-range elimination .
  · Consistent DPS: Against tanky lineups, switching to Nibiru (SMG) provides high sustained damage (DPS) to shred through front-liners .
  · Area Control: Using Bennett (Bazooka) provides massive AoE damage and slows, which is perfect for zoning enemies during Lord fights or objective takes .
  · Double Ultimate: She can use one ultimate, quickly swap weapons with her first skill, and then unleash a second different ultimate in a single team fight .
· Objective Control:
  · Turret Pressure: She can safely attack turrets from outside their range using Renner or Bennett, making her an excellent split pusher .
  · High Carry Potential: Due to her damage output and safety, she has a high capacity to "carry" games, especially in solo rank pushes .
· Good Against:
  · Beatrix is strong against immobile heroes or short-range fighters who struggle to close the gap against her Renner (Sniper) .
  · Heroes like Brody and Clint (despite being even matchups) can be poked down by a skilled Beatrix player who manages her range well
hero.hero_weakness:
Hard Counter Marksmen: In the laning phase (Gold Lane), Beatrix struggles against specific marksmen who have high early-game damage or superior poke.

  · Clint: He is considered a direct counter. His Quick Draw skill allows him to poke Beatrix from a safe distance with high damage in the early game, and his ultimate can easily secure a kill once he reaches level 4 .

  · Brody: He has strong early-game damage that rivals Beatrix's, making him a dangerous opponent in lane .

  · Moskov: His high attack speed and burst damage, combined with his teleport skill, allow him to disrupt Beatrix's farming and win trades in the Gold Lane .

  · Claude: His mobility and skill Art of Thievery can reduce Beatrix's movement and attack speed, making it hard for her to dominate the lane .

· Item Counter: When facing Beatrix, marksmen can buy Wind of Nature. Its active skill grants temporary immunity to physical damage, which can completely negate Beatrix's ultimate or burst combo in a 1v1 fight .

spell.spell_desc:
Instantly teleports your hero a short distance in the designated direction.

· Why take it: Beatrix has no reliable escape ability. Flicker compensates for this weakness by providing instant mobility to dodge assassins, escape ganks, or just give up if it's Hilda and martis on active ultimate

hero.hero_overview: Beatrix is a marksman in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang known for her four different weapons: Renner (sniper), Bennett (rocket launcher), Wesker (shotgun), and Nibiru (SMG). She is a high burst-damage marksman who can adjust her playstyle depending on the situation. She is usually played in the Gold Lane and focuses on farming early to dominate mid to late game.
hero.hero_strength:
Very high burst damage, especially with Renner (sniper) and Wesker (shotgun).
Flexible playstyle because of four weapons.
Strong poke in lane using Renner.
Great team fight damage with Bennett (AoE rockets).
Strong in mid to late game when farmed.
Can outplay enemies with proper positioning and weapon switching.
hero.hero_weakness:
Very dependent on positioning (no dash skill).
Weak against assassins and heavy crowd control.
Needs good aim and fast hand switching weapons.
Can fall behind if bullied early game.
Squishy and easy to burst if caught.

spell.spell_desc: You can use flicker when using skills I suprising enemies

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Tigreal ultimate followed by Beatrix ultimate in late game can wipe out entire enemy. You don't need anyone else. You can use any heroes that can set enemies very well.
Mages like Zetian and Vaxena can help Beatrix stun the enemies. Then Beatrix can finish target easily.
This is a kind of troll build so it can fail. You can try this in classic. Novaria's ult can expand enemies hit bar so it is very helpful for Beatrix sniper weapon.

counters.counter_desc: All assassins are counter of all marksmen. Not only Hayabusa but most of the assassins can shut her down if not played well. And burst mage like Eudora, Vale can also easily kill her.
If marksmen like Clint, Claude and Granger get fed in early game, then he can be troublesome in late game.

dominants.dominant_desc:
In early game, either you should play very safe or very aggressive. If enemy marksman has full support from tank or mage and jungler are rotating frequently, play very safe .Quickly clear the minions and stay inside the turret. But if enemy marksmen is alone or your team is also rotating, then don't hesitate and play aggressively. Beatrix can defeat any marksman in early game if she gets support. You can target enemy marksman and freeze the lane. Or you can clear fast, take the gold crab and help other lanes. But in any case , don't die in early game. The first five minutes of the game decide your whole gameplay in many cases.

And for build path, build magic blade at first because it gives you early lifesteal and shield at low HP. Then complete the boots and immediately go for blade of despair.

For weapons, use missile and machine gun(don't know the names). You can also use sniper instead of machine gun but missile is the most. Because it can fast clear minions and it has long range so you can poke enemies.

Once you get your ultimate and blade of despair, your hunting time begins. Clear the minions, hid in the bush and wait for enemy marksman, mage or jungler. Once anyone shoes up, immediately use missile weapon ultimate and one basic attack. Then quickly switch to machine gun and use it's ultimate or basic attack depending on their HP. If they try to run, use second skill or flicker to chase. Most of enemy marksman and mage can be deleted instantly by this combo. But if they survive and try to fight back, don't back down because you still have shield from magic blade. But if your missile ultimate misses, then be very careful. Back down if needed. Don't die in any case.

Once you get most of your items, you become a demon. You can one shot squishy from your any ultimate. You would have great damage that even tanks fear you. The combo is same, hid in the bush use missile ultimate, followed by other ultimate. This time you can choose any weapon instead of machine gun. You can use shotgun to instantly burst squishy. Use basic attack, ultimate and basic attack combo. Or use sniper basic attack and ultimate. You can kill with sniper ultimate only if we'll fed. In teamfight, missile and machine gun mode are the best. Split push if you're losing, stea lord with your ult or deal damage while staying in backline. But don't move without vision of enemies. Because no matter how much strong you are, enemy mage or assassin can one shot you in late game and they will definitely ambush you or prioritize you in teamfight. So stay safe and let the tanks take damage.

emblems.emblem_desc: I recommend using marksman emblem for Beatrix because it gives attack speed and lifesteal.For talents, you can use swift as it gives you attack speed. Next you can choose among weapon master, bargain hunter or wilderness blessing. Bargain hunter is helpful when enemy is early game hero. Wilderness blessing is useful when enemy has high rotation potential. Weapon master is useful in every situation so you can choose it if you are beginner. For core talent, you can choose between quantum charge and weakness finder. But quantum charge is best for her as it gives additional movement speed and restores some hp which is helpful during fights.

equips.equip_desc: This is one of the best build for Beatrix which can be useful in any situation. The main core item is blade of despair because it gives Beatrix maximum damage. Rapid boots because it gives movement speed. Malefic gun for range and penetration. You can also build rose gold meteor as it gives lifesteal and shield. You can use demon hunter sword if enemy has many tanky heroes or you can switch to Haas claws if enemy are squishy. You can use wind of nature if enemies are more physical damage dealers, sea halbert if enemies have more regenerative or lifesteal heroes or you can use immortality if enemies are killing you instantly.

hero.hero_overview: Beatrix is an all rounder marksman. Her four different forms of weapons make her unique from others. She can choose to be an assassin, a sniper, a burst hero or continuous damage dealer according to the situation. Once she gets all her items, she becomes unstoppable. She can defeat almost anyone. Once understood the gun mechanics and builds according to the situation, I promise you won't want to switch to other marksman.
hero.hero_strength: The main strength of Beatrix is the weapons. Unlike other marksman, Beatrix can switch between different types which helps her face all types of enemies.
hero.hero_weakness: The main weakness of Beatrix and any other marksman is hp. Beatrix is a squishy hero. So if you don't become careful, enemy mage or assassin can easily burst you before you take any action.

data.title: Beatrix

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Perfect synergy. Their AoE crowd control locks enemies in place, letting Beatrix land Renner snipes or Bennett rockets for massive burst damage.

counters.counter_desc: Hanabi lacks mobility and is an easy target for Beatrixs sniper or rocket poke
Beatrixs sustained DPS outshines Grangers limited bullet burst in extended fights.
Sabers single-target suppression easily deletes Beatrix, especially if she has no Wind of Nature available.

dominants.dominant_desc:
1. Always stay behind your frontline. Use Renner (sniper) and Bennett (rocket) to poke safely from a distance. Clear waves quickly with Bennett before rotating. Avoid overextending alone, especially when assassins like Ling or Saber are missing.
2. Position at the backline and switch guns depending on the situation. Use Renner for high single-target burst, Wesker for close-range damage when enemies dive, and Bennett for AoE damage in clustered fights. Wait for enemies to use their CC skills before stepping forward. Activate Wind of Nature when enemy marksmen or assassins dive on you, and rely on Immortality or Rose Gold Meteor for survival in late-game clashes.
3. Do not rush into fights; Beatrix excels when she stays patient, kites enemies, and punishes mistakes with her high burst and flexible gun swaps.

emblems.emblem_desc:
The Assassin Emblem is ideal for Beatrix because it matches her core build and playstyle.
	1.	Rupture increases penetration, making items like Blade of Despair and Malefic Roar deal stronger burst damage.
	2.	Weapon Master boosts the attack power gained from items such as Sea Halberd and Rose Gold Meteor, maximizing her late-game scaling.
	3.	Quantum Charge provides healing and movement speed, helping her sustain during trades and reposition safely until she can activate Wind of Nature or rely on defensive items like Immortality.

equips.equip_desc: The chosen build maximizes Beatrixs strengths as a flexible marksman who can poke, burst, and sustain in fights.

hero.hero_overview: Beatrix is a gold lane marksman with unique weapon mastery, allowing her to switch between four guns: Renner (sniper), Wesker (shotgun), Bennett (rocket launcher), and Nibiru (SMG). She is one of the most versatile damage dealers in MLBB, capable of long-range poke, close-range burst, wave clear, and sustained DPS. With proper mechanics and positioning, Beatrix can dominate both early lane trades and late-game team fights.
hero.hero_strength:
1.	Versatile playstyle with four different weapons.
	2.	High burst damage with Renner and Wesker.
	3.	Consistent DPS using Nibiru.
	4.	Strong lane control and early pressure.
	5.	Effective wave clear and zoning with Bennett.
	6.	Scales well into the late game with items.
hero.hero_weakness:
1.	High skill cap and requires good aim.
	2.	No mobility, making her vulnerable to assassins.
	3.	Very squishy and easy to burst if mispositioned.
	4.	Needs correct weapon setup to maximize ultimate.
	5.	Item dependent and weak when behind in gold.
	6.	Struggles against heavy crowd control and dive heroes.

data.recommend: If youre looking for a flexible marksman who can poke, burst, and carry team fights, this Beatrix guide is for you! 🫶 It covers her best emblem setup, item build, phase strategies, and counters

emblems.emblem_desc: good emblem build depends on the enemy team's composition. If they have a lot of tanks, the Assassin Emblem with the Weapon Master talent is recommended for the increased physical penetration. If they have a more squishy team, the Marksman Emblem with the Weapon Master talent is a better choice for the extra physical damage.

good emblem build depends on the enemy team's composition. If they have a lot of tanks, the Assassin Emblem with the Weapon Master talent is recommended for the increased physical penetration. If they have a more squishy team, the Marksman Emblem with the Weapon Master talent is a better choice for the extra physical damage.

hero.hero_overview: Beatrix is a popular marksman hero with a unique skill set. She has a passive ability that allows her to switch between four different weapons, each with its own unique attack style. Beatrix's skills and abilities allow her to be a powerful damage dealer from a distance, while also providing mobility and crowd control options.
hero.hero_strength: Beatrix, depending on the context, is either a powerful character with high stats in a video game or a strong character in the The Eminence in Shadow series
hero.hero_weakness: Beatrix, a marksman in Mobile Legends, is weak against heroes with high burst damage and strong single-target crowd control (CC). Her reliance on basic attacks for lifesteal makes her vulnerable to stun-locking, interrupting her regeneration and making her more susceptible to being burst down.
